name: Color Personality
description: What colour matches your personality? Discover the hue that reflects who you truly are.

red.title: Red — The Powerhouse
red.description: You are bold, passionate and driven. Red is the colour of action, energy and ambition. You don't wait for things to happen — you make them happen. Your presence is felt the moment you walk into a room.
red.strengths: Your determination and courage mean you achieve what others only dream about. You inspire people around you to raise their own standards.
red.challenges: Your intensity can sometimes overwhelm others. Practising patience and active listening will make you an even more powerful force.

blue.title: Blue — The Deep Thinker
blue.description: You are calm, thoughtful and deeply loyal. Blue is the colour of wisdom, trust and depth. You think before you speak, feel deeply and value genuine connection over surface-level interaction.
blue.strengths: Your reliability and wisdom make you the person everyone turns to when things get serious. Your clarity and depth of thinking are genuine gifts.
blue.challenges: Your high standards and tendency toward perfectionism can hold you back. Embrace good enough sometimes — done is better than perfect.

yellow.title: Yellow — The Bright Spirit
yellow.description: You are optimistic, creative and wonderfully expressive. Yellow is the colour of sunshine, joy and innovation. You light up any room and see possibilities where others see walls.
yellow.strengths: Your positivity is infectious. You have the rare gift of making difficult situations feel manageable and inspiring others to see the bright side.
yellow.challenges: Your enthusiasm sometimes leads to starting more than you finish. Building structure around your creativity will help you achieve your brilliant ideas.

green.title: Green — The Grounded Nurturer
green.description: You are balanced, compassionate and deeply empathetic. Green is the colour of growth, harmony and healing. You are the anchor in your relationships — steady, caring and always thinking about others.
green.strengths: You create genuine safety and harmony wherever you go. People feel accepted and valued in your presence, and you help communities thrive.
green.challenges: You give so much to others that you sometimes neglect your own needs. Learning to prioritise yourself is not selfish — it's essential.

questions.question: When you walk into a party where you don't know many people, you typically:
options: Dive right in and start introducing yourself to everyone
Observe the room, find someone interesting and have one deep conversation
Head for the most fun-looking group and get the energy going
Find a comfortable corner and wait for someone to approach you first

questions.question: Your friends would most likely describe you as:
options: Confident and go-getting — always chasing the next goal
Wise and reliable — the one everyone comes to for advice
Fun and spontaneous — guaranteed to make everyone laugh
Kind and steady — always there when someone needs support

questions.question: When facing a difficult decision, you:
options: Trust your gut and act fast — hesitation costs opportunity
Analyse every angle carefully before committing
Brainstorm creatively and look for unexpected solutions
Think about how it affects everyone involved before deciding

questions.question: What is your biggest motivator?
options: Winning, succeeding and achieving big things
Understanding the world deeply and getting things right
Having fun, being creative and experiencing life fully
Making others happy and keeping the peace

questions.question: Under pressure, you tend to:
options: Take charge and push through — pressure brings out your best
Slow down, think carefully and make a methodical plan
Look for a creative or unconventional way out
Seek support from others and lean on your network

questions.question: Which of these words resonates most with you?
options: Ambition
Depth
Freedom
Harmony

questions.question: Your ideal weekend looks like:
options: A challenging hike, a competitive game or smashing a goal
A good book, a documentary and time to think
An art class, a festival or trying something completely new
A relaxed day with people you love, good food and no rush

questions.question: When something goes wrong in a project, your instinct is to:
options: Immediately take action and fix it — no time for dwelling
Figure out exactly what went wrong and learn from it
Reframe the setback as a creative opportunity
Make sure the whole team is okay before solving the problem
